subject: Computer Networking
title: What Is a Modem?
description: A quick win: understand What Is a Modem?.

hook.text: Before your router can share the internet, something has to receive the signal. That's the modem's job.

buildup.text: A modem converts ISP signals into data your home network can use. It speaks two languages.

discovery.text: Cable modems decode coaxial. DSL modems decode phone lines. Fiber modems convert light to electrical data.

twist.text: Modem means 'modulate-demodulate.' It translates signals between two physical formats.

climax.text: Many ISPs combine modem and router in one device. Simpler setup, but less control for you.

punchline.text: The modem translates between your ISP's wire and your network.

quiz.question: What does the word 'modem' stand for?
